reasoning: Chancroid is an infection caused by Haemophilus ducreyi, which is easily treatable with antibiotics, and its global incidence has steeply declined in recent years. There is little to no commercial incentive or active large-scale clinical development for a chancroid vaccine right now. Furthermore, vaccine development and approval processes take many years. Given that a vaccine did not exist as of late October 2025, it is essentially impossible for one to be developed, approved, and updated on Wikipedia within the next 1 to 3 months. The probabilities are kept at the lower bound permitted by the prompt rules (0.05) and slightly increased over time to account for growing uncertainty, such as the remote chance of temporary Wikipedia vandalism or definition changes.
